claims.claim: The core historical matched-candidate universe contains 59,816 opportunities across 18,072 matches.
Correct match-specific residual alignment beats 4,000 baseline-preserving circular-shift placebos for selected and incremental closing log-CLV, with empirical upper-tail p=0.00025.
A one-standard-deviation increase in residual uplift is associated with +0.004430 same-book closing log-CLV, with event-cluster 95% interval [+0.003316,+0.005515].

The closing log-CLV slope is positive for all eight tested bookmakers, all three selected outcomes, and all four historical cutoffs; every leave-one-book-out lower confidence bound remains above zero.
At matched 5% capacity, the residual policy records +0.565% historical ROI versus -0.747% for the raw reference, but both standalone and paired uncertainty intervals cross zero.
required_literals: confidence interval did not clear zero

claims.claim: Residual-minus-raw point return is positive in 60 of 64 frozen execution cells, yet no preregistered practical envelope clears paired uncertainty and adversarial rejection removes standalone profitability.
required_literals: every paired confidence interval crossed zero

claims.claim: Historical practical-envelope return uplift is positive for home in 4/4 mechanisms, draw in 0/4, and away in 1/4; removing home makes uplift negative in three of four mechanisms.
required_literals: home incremental point return positive in 4/4

claims.claim: Residual standalone closing-line relative value is positive with bootstrap lower bounds above zero in all 12 outcome-by-mechanism cells, while away closing-value uplift often fails to become realized return.
required_literals: sign divergence in three of four mechanisms

claims.claim: The prospective adapter exhibits material timing-support and peer-panel breadth shifts, motivating separately activated parallel adapters rather than retroactive alteration of the original stream.
The prospective shadow bundle contains twelve bookmaker-identity-free models with pinned runtime and model checksums, and emits research-only scores for fixed bookmaker/outcome identities.
